attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

content.thinking:
Now I'm seeing more detail about how Target/GoldTarget symbols are handled during symbol drops. The code shows two paths:

1. If there's an existing target spine from the same column, it tweens to follow the drop
2. If there's no existing spine, a new one is created on landing via ReelStopEffect

In both cases, when created or when landing, the code plays "Stop" → "Idle" animations, but I still don't see any "In" animation being played. This is still investigation phase. I need to wait for either the actual bug being identified or a fix being implemented.

content.text: I'm observing the session reading the symbol drop logic in SlotReels.ts (lines 1895-1985). The code shows how Target/GoldTarget symbols are handled during drops - either reusing existing spine nodes or creating new ones on landing. Both paths play "Stop" → "Idle" but no "In" animation. Still in investigation phase. Waiting for the root cause identification or fix.

{\\n            const pos = targetData.Pos;\\n            const col = Math.floor(pos / Game_Define.ROW);\\n            const row = pos % Game_Define.ROW;\\n            const worldPos = this.GetSymbolNodeWorldPos(col, row);\\n\\n            this.SetSymbolShow(col, row, false);\\n\\n            // 用停輪時的同一個 node 播 Active，播完歸還\\n            const position = Common.GetPositionFromColRow(col, row);\\n            const stopNodeInfo = this.m_targetStopNodes.get(position);\\n            if (stopNodeInfo) {\\n                const spine = stopNodeInfo.node.getComponent(BaseSpine);\\n                if (spine) {\\n                    spine.PlayAnimation(\\\"Active\\\", spine.Spine, false).then(() => {\\n                        stopNodeInfo.node.active = false;\\n                        PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().ReturnNode(stopNodeInfo.prefabKey, stopNodeInfo.node);\\n                    });\\n                }\\n                this.m_targetStopNodes.delete(position);\\n            } else {\\n                // fallback：沒有停輪 node 時從 pool 取\\n                const symbolNode = PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().GetNode(symbolPrefabKey);\\n                if (symbolNode) {\\n                    const spine = symbolNode.getComponent(BaseSpine);\\n                    if (spine) {\\n                        symbolNode.setParent(this.m_effectNodes[Game_Define.LAYERS.SCATTER]);\\n                        symbolNode.setWorldPosition(worldPos);\\n                        symbolNode.active = true;\\n                        spine.PlayAnimation(\\\"Active\\\", spine.Spine, false).then(() => {\\n                            symbolNode.active = false;\\n                            PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().ReturnNode(symbolPrefabKey, symbolNode);\\n                        });\\n                    } else {\\n                        PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().ReturnNode(symbolPrefabKey, symbolNode);\\n                    }\\n                }\\n            }\\n\\n            // Target_Explode 音效提早 0.5s\\n            Define.Wait(this.m_gameView, 0.5).then(() => {\\n                AudioManager.Play(AudioManager.AudioClips.Target_Explode);\\n            });\\n            // Target_Explode spine（立即播）\\n            {\\n                const explodeNode = PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().GetNode(TARGET_EXPLODE_PREFAB_KEY);\\n                if (explodeNode) {\\n                    const baseSpine = explodeNode.getComponent(BaseSpine);\\n                    if (baseSpine) {\\n                        explodeNode.setParent(this.m_effectNodes[Game_Define.LAYERS.TARGET]);\\n                        explodeNode.setWorldPosition(worldPos);\\n                        explodeNode.active = true;\\n                        baseSpine.PlayAnimation(explodeAnimName, baseSpine.Spine, false).then(() => {\\n                            explodeNode.active = false;\\n                            PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().ReturnNode(TARGET_EXPLODE_PREFAB_KEY, explodeNode);\\n                        });\\n                    } else {\\n                        PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().ReturnNode(TARGET_EXPLODE_PREFAB_KEY, explodeNode);\\n                    }\\n                }\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        await Define.Wait(this.m_gameView, 1.5);\\n    }\\n\\n    async ShowMultiplyFlyEffect(pos: number, winValue: string, isLeft: boolean) {\\n        let targetPos = this.m_gameView.SlotReels.GetMiddleSymbolEmptyNodeFromPosition(pos).getWorldPosition();\\n        let multiplyFlyNode = PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().GetNode(MULTI_FLY_PREFAB_KEY);\\n        if (!multiplyFlyNode) {\\n            error(\\\"multiplyFlyNode\\\");\\n            return;\\n        }\\n        let multiplyFly = multiplyFlyNode.getComponent(MultiplyFlyUI);\\n        if (!multiplyFly) {\\n            error(\\\"multiplyFly\\\");\\n            return;\\n        }\\n        multiplyFlyNode.setParent(this.m_effectNodes[Game_Define.LAYERS.MULTI_FLY]);\\n        multiplyFlyNode.setWorldPosition(targetPos);\\n        multiplyFlyNode.active = true;\\n\\n        let promises: Promise<void>[] = [];\\n        promises.push( new Promise(\\n            async ( resolve ) =>\\n            {\\n                multiplyFly.SetWinValue(winValue);\\n                await multiplyFly.ShowWinValue(isLeft);\\n                resolve();\\n            }\\n        ) );\\n        await Promise.all( promises );\\n        PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().ReturnNode(MULTI_FLY_PREFAB_KEY, multiplyFlyNode);\\n    }\\n\\n    /**@ch 顯示 ODD 靜止不回收，返回 node 供後續更新*/\\n    async ShowStaticOdds(pos: number, text: string, isLeft: boolean): Promise<Node> {\\n        let targetPos = this.m_gameView.SlotReels.GetMiddleSymbolEmptyNodeFromPosition(pos).getWorldPosition();\\n        let multiplyFlyNode = PrefabPoolManager.GetInstance().GetNode(MULTI_FLY_PREFAB_KEY);\\n        if (!multiplyFlyNode) { error(\\\"multiplyFlyNode\\\"); return null; }\",\"numLines\":140,\"startLine\":660,\"totalLines\":1929}}"</outcome>

content.thinking:
I'm observing the session reading the `StartFallSymbol` method (lines 3138-3228). This is the method that handles symbol drops during cascade. The code shows that symbols drop and land, but there's no "In" animation being triggered for Target/GoldTarget symbols after they land.

The investigation has revealed:
1. Scatter symbols get `ReelStopEffect` + `PlayScatterEnterAnimation` (the "In" animation)
2. Target/GoldTarget symbols only get `ReelStopEffect` (which plays "Stop" → "Idle" animation)
3. There's no equivalent "PlayTargetEnterAnimation" or similar call for Target symbols

This is still investigation work. I need to wait for the actual fix to be implemented.

The edit adds diagnostic logging in the NearWin branch to detect when Target/GoldTarget symbols land but don't trigger the "IN" animation. This is adding instrumentation to identify the bug, not fixing it yet.

I should wait for the actual fix implementation before creating an observation.

aiTitle: 金色標靶播放問題診斷

content.thinking:
Now I'm seeing diagnostic logs being added to the EnterPlate (refill) method in SlotReels.ts. This adds logging to track whether Target symbols are getting their "Stop" animation when they land during refills (tile matching/cascade).

The logs are:
1. When spine is found and plays "Stop" animation - logs "播IN(Stop)" (playing IN)
2. When BaseSpine component is missing - logs "無IN BaseSpine元件缺失" (no IN, BaseSpine component missing)
3. When targetSpineNode is null, falls back to ReelStopEffect - logs "targetSpineNode=null→改走ReelStopEffect"

This is still investigation instrumentation to diagnose why GoldTarget "sometimes" doesn't play the IN animation. Still waiting for the actual fix.

content.text: I'm observing more diagnostic logging being added to the `EnterPlate` method (the refill/cascade path) in SlotReels.ts. This instruments the Target symbol stopCb to track whether the spine node exists, whether the BaseSpine component is present, and which animation path is taken. This is detailed diagnostic work to identify the root cause, not the fix yet. Waiting for the actual bugfix implementation.

未設置（提前到 method 開頭，避免 EH-3 重複報 error）\\n        if ( !this.m_wantedSymbolSpinePrefab )\\n        {\\n            warn( '[EffectPlate] SpawnAndPlayWantedSymbolIn: m_wantedSymbolSpinePrefab 未設置，跳過' );\\n
